SEARCH
Package core

Class LoadBalancer

java.lang.Object
core.LoadBalancer
public class LoadBalancer extends Object
Manages server load balancing, distribution, failover, and cloud scaling. This class implements multiple load balancing strategies, including:
  • Round Robin: Distributes data evenly across servers.
  • Least Loaded: Prioritizes servers with the lowest load.
  • Weighted Distribution: Allocates data based on server weights.
  • Consistent Hashing: Ensures consistent server selection for data keys.
  • Capacity-Aware Distribution: Considers server capacity and current load.
  • Predictive Load Balancing: Predicts future load for smarter distribution.

Key Features:

  • Dynamically manages active servers.
  • Monitors server health and triggers failover for unhealthy servers.
  • Supports AWS Cloud auto-scaling integration via CloudManager.
  • Implements priority queue-based load management.
  • Uses a monitoring thread (ServerMonitor) for real-time updates.
  • Provides structured logging for alerts and system state.

    serverMap
    Map of server IDs to server instances, synchronized for thread safety.

  • Field Details

    • servers

      public final List<Server> servers
      List of active servers, thread-safe with synchronized access.

    • serverMap

      public final Map<String,Server> serverMap
      Map of server IDs to server instances, synchronized for thread safety.

  • Constructor Details

    • LoadBalancer

      public LoadBalancer()
      Initializes a new LoadBalancer with monitoring enabled. Creates a ServerMonitor instance and starts it in a separate thread to continuously monitor server health and metrics.

  • Method Details

    • initializeCloud

      public void initializeCloud(String accessKey, String secretKey, String region, int minServers, int maxServers)
      Initializes cloud integration with AWS and starts cloud-based scaling. Creates a new CloudManager instance if none exists, using the provided AWS credentials and region. Initializes the specified number of cloud servers and starts auto-scaling.
      Parameters:
      accessKey - AWS access key for authentication
      secretKey - AWS secret key for authentication
      region - AWS region (e.g., "us-east-1")
      minServers - Minimum number of cloud servers to maintain
      maxServers - Maximum number of cloud servers to scale to
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if cloud initialization fails

    • getCloudManager

      public CloudManager getCloudManager()
      Retrieves the CloudManager instance for AWS integration.
      Returns:
      the CloudManager instance, or null if not initialized

    • addServer

      public void addServer(Server server)
      Adds a new server to the load balancer. The server is added to the list of active servers, the server map, the load queue, and the consistent hashing ring for distribution.
      Parameters:
      server - the server to be added

    • checkServerHealth

      public void checkServerHealth()
      Checks and updates the health status of all servers. Servers are marked unhealthy if their CPU, memory, or disk usage reaches 100%, or if they are already marked unhealthy. Unhealthy servers are collected and passed to the failover process.

    • roundRobin

      public Map<String,Double> roundRobin(double totalData)
      Distributes data evenly across healthy servers using the round-robin strategy. Each healthy server receives an equal share of the total data, and the current distribution is updated accordingly.
      Parameters:
      totalData - the total data to distribute (in GB)
      Returns:
      a map of server IDs to allocated data amounts

    • leastLoaded

      public Map<String,Double> leastLoaded(double totalData)
      Distributes data to the least loaded healthy servers. Servers are sorted by their load score, and data is allocated starting with the least loaded server until the total data is distributed.
      Parameters:
      totalData - the total data to distribute (in GB)
      Returns:
      a map of server IDs to allocated data amounts

    • weightedDistribution

      public Map<String,Double> weightedDistribution(double totalData)
      Distributes data based on server weights. Each healthy server receives a portion of the total data proportional to its weight relative to the total weight of all healthy servers.
      Parameters:
      totalData - the total data to distribute (in GB)
      Returns:
      a map of server IDs to allocated data amounts

    • consistentHashing

      public Map<String,Double> consistentHashing(double totalData, int numKeys)
      Distributes data using consistent hashing. Data keys are hashed and mapped to servers using a consistent hashing ring, ensuring consistent server selection for the same data key.
      Parameters:
      totalData - the total data to distribute (in GB)
      numKeys - the number of data keys to distribute
      Returns:
      a map of server IDs to allocated data amounts

    • capacityAware

      public Map<String,Double> capacityAware(double totalData)
      Distributes data based on server capacity and current load. Servers are sorted by their load-to-capacity ratio, and data is allocated based on available capacity, prioritizing servers with more capacity.
      Parameters:
      totalData - the total data to distribute (in GB)
      Returns:
      a map of server IDs to allocated data amounts

    • predictiveLoadBalancing

      public Map<String,Double> predictiveLoadBalancing(double totalData)
      Distributes data based on predicted future load. Predicts future load by scaling the current load score by 1.1, then allocates data based on remaining capacity, prioritizing servers with lower predicted load.
      Parameters:
      totalData - the total data to distribute (in GB)
      Returns:
      a map of server IDs to allocated data amounts

    • importServerLogs

      public void importServerLogs(String filename, String format)
      Imports server logs asynchronously from a file in the specified format. Uses the Utils class to import server logs in CSV or JSON format, running the import operation in a separate thread via the executor service.
      Parameters:
      filename - the path to the log file
      format - the format of the log file ("csv" or "json")

    • exportReport

      public void exportReport(String filename, String format) throws IOException
      Exports server status and alerts to a report file. Delegates to the Utils class to export server data and alerts in the specified format.
      Parameters:
      filename - the path to the output report file
      format - the format of the report ("csv" or "json")
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs during file writing

    • shutdown

      public void shutdown()
      Gracefully shuts down the load balancer. Stops the server monitor, terminates the executor service, shuts down cloud resources if cloud integration is enabled, and logs the shutdown process.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the shutdown process is interrupted

    • logAlert

      public void logAlert(String alertMsg)
      Logs an alert message to the alert log.
      Parameters:
      alertMsg - the alert message to log

    • getServers

      public List<Server> getServers()
      Retrieves a copy of the list of servers. Returns a copy to prevent external modification of the internal list.
      Returns:
      a list of servers

    • getAlertLog

      public List<String> getAlertLog()
      Retrieves a copy of the list of logged alerts. Returns a copy to prevent external modification of the internal list.
      Returns:
      a list of alert messages

    • getServerMap

      public Map<String,Server> getServerMap()
      Retrieves the map of server IDs to server objects.
      Returns:
      the server map

    • updateMetricsFromCloud

      public void updateMetricsFromCloud()
      Updates server metrics from cloud data if cloud integration is enabled. Delegates to the CloudManager to fetch and update metrics from AWS CloudWatch.

    • scaleCloudServers

      public void scaleCloudServers(int desiredCapacity)
      Scales the number of cloud servers to the desired capacity. Delegates to the CloudManager to adjust the number of active cloud servers.
      Parameters:
      desiredCapacity - the desired number of servers
